text.title: Django Import Anti-Patterns
text.summary: Unaliased cross-app imports, wildcard imports, from __future__ + DRF introspection, and PEP 810 syntax in 3.12 code.

antipattern.description: Importing model classes directly from other apps without aliases: `from apps.users.models import User`.
antipattern.reason: Direct unaliased cross-app imports create circular dependency risks and name collision hazards. When two apps both have a `User` model, the last import wins — silently. Use `from apps.users import models as user_models` and reference `user_models.User` everywhere.

antipattern.description: Using `from app.models import *` or `from app.views import *` anywhere in a Django project.
antipattern.reason: Wildcard imports make it impossible to determine where a name comes from without running the code. They also import private names (`_internal`), pollute the module namespace, and confuse both human readers and type checkers. Django's autoloading already handles `admin.py`, `apps.py`, etc. — wildcards add no value.

antipattern.description: Adding `from __future__ import annotations` to DRF serializer or view files that use DRF field introspection or `drf-spectacular` schema generation.
antipattern.reason: DRF uses `get_type_hints()` for serializer field resolution. `from __future__ import annotations` defers all annotations to strings, which breaks DRF's introspection and drf-spectacular's schema generation. The annotations become unparseable strings at the point where DRF expects actual type objects. Verify DRF compatibility before adding `__future__` annotations to serializer files.

antipattern.description: Using `lazy from heavy_module import HeavyClass` syntax (PEP 810) on Python 3.12 or 3.13 codebases.
antipattern.reason: PEP 810 lazy import syntax is not available until Python 3.14+. Using it on 3.12/3.13 causes `SyntaxError` at import time — not at the usage site. The current alternative (function-scoped import or `importlib.import_module()`) achieves the same effect without version risk.

example.note: Lazy import on Python 3.12/3.13 without PEP 810 syntax

antipattern.description: Using `from ...users.models import User` or other multi-dot relative imports across app boundaries.
antipattern.reason: Multi-dot relative imports break on any directory restructuring and create fragile coupling to the physical directory layout. They also confuse readers about where the module lives. Replace with absolute cross-app imports: `from apps.users import models as user_models`.
